revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 雷電神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=さいたま市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]][[今羽町]]154 |地図 = |祭神 = [[雷神|大雷神]]、[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''雷電神社'''（らいでんじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ[[別当寺]]の「三明院」が[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]に創建されていることから、その頃に創建されたものと推測される。三明院は[[修験道]][[本山派]]の寺院であったが、[[明治]]初期の[[神仏分離]]により、[[廃寺]]に追い込まれた<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、324-325p</ref>。
[[1873年]]（明治6年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられ、[[1886年]]（明治19年）に三明院が別当寺を務めていた「氷川社」を合祀した<ref name="神社"></ref>。そのため、現在の当社の[[鳥居]]の[[扁額]]には「雷電神社（右）氷川神社（左）」と書かれている。
== 交通アクセス == *[[今羽駅]]より徒歩6分。

== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年

It enshrines {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神}} (Ōraijin) and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under the Modern Shrine Ranking System. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. However, its former {{..."
revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(さいたま市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kita-ku, Saitama|ja|北区 (さいたま市)}}, Saitama City, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神}} (Ōraijin) and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. However, its former {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}}, "Sanmyōin," was established during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, leading to speculation that the shrine was founded around the same time. Sanmyōin was a temple of the {{ill|Honzan-ha|ja|本山派}} sect of {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}, but it was abolished during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} reforms of the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}.<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998, pp. 324–325.</ref>
In 1873, under the Modern Shrine Ranking System, Raiden Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社). In 1886, it absorbed the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|ja|氷川神社}} previously managed by Sanmyōin as its bettō-ji.<ref name="jinja"></ref> The shrine's torii plaque now bears the inscriptions "Raiden Shrine (right)" and "Hikawa Shrine (left)," reflecting this historical merger.
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kombu Station|ja|今羽駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998.
{{Pref-stub|pref=Saitama}} {{Shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Raiden Shrine}} [[Category:Raiden Shrines]] [[Category:Hikawa Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kita-ku, Saitama]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[ja:雷電神社]]

revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(さいたま市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kita-ku, Saitama|ja|北区 (さいたま市)}}, Saitama City, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神}} (Ōraijin) and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. However, its former {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}}, "Sanmyōin," was established during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, leading to speculation that the shrine was founded around the same time. Sanmyōin was a temple of the {{ill|Honzan-ha|ja|本山派}} sect of {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}, but it was abolished during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} reforms of the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}.<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998, pp. 324–325.</ref>
In 1873, under the Modern Shrine Ranking System, Raiden Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社). In 1886, it absorbed the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|ja|氷川神社}} previously managed by Sanmyōin as its bettō-ji.<ref name="jinja"></ref> The shrine's torii plaque now bears the inscriptions "Raiden Shrine (right)" and "Hikawa Shrine (left)," reflecting this historical merger.
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kombu Station|ja|今羽駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998.
{{Pref-stub|pref=Saitama}} {{Shinto-stub}}

[[ja:雷電神社_(さいたま市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kita-ku, Saitama|ja|北区 (さいたま市)}}, Saitama City, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神}} (Ōraijin) and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.

However, its former {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}}, "Sanmyōin," was established during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, leading to speculation that the shrine was founded around the same time. Sanmyōin was a temple of the {{ill|Honzan-ha|ja|本山派}} sect of {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}, but it was abolished during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} reforms of the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}.<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998, pp. 324–325.</ref>
In 1873, under the Modern Shrine Ranking System, Raiden Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社). In 1886, it absorbed the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|ja|氷川神社}} previously managed by Sanmyōin as its bettō-ji.<ref name="jinja"></ref> The shrine's torii plaque now bears the inscriptions "Raiden Shrine (right)" and "Hikawa Shrine (left)," reflecting this historical merger.

[[ja:雷電神社_(さいたま市)]]
'''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kita-ku, Saitama|ja|北区 (さいたま市)}}, Saitama City, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Raijin|ja|雷神}} (Ōraijin) and {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞嗚尊}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. However, its former {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}}, "Sanmyōin," was established during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, leading to speculation that the shrine was founded around the same time. Sanmyōin was a temple of the {{ill|Honzan-ha|ja|本山派}} sect of {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}, but it was abolished during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} reforms of the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}.<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998, pp. 324–325.</ref>
In 1873, under the Modern Shrine Ranking System, Raiden Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社). In 1886, it absorbed the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|ja|氷川神社}} previously managed by Sanmyōin as its bettō-ji.<ref name="jinja"></ref> The shrine's torii plaque now bears the inscriptions "Raiden Shrine (right)" and "Hikawa Shrine (left)," reflecting this historical merger.
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kombu Station|ja|今羽駅}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998.

In 1886, it absorbed the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|ja|氷川神社}} previously managed by Sanmyōin as its bettō-ji.<ref name="jinja"></ref> The shrine's torii plaque now bears the inscriptions "Raiden Shrine (right)" and "Hikawa Shrine (left)," reflecting this historical merger.

In 1886, it absorbed the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|ja|氷川神社|12=draft}} previously managed by Sanmyōin as its bettō-ji.<ref name="jinja"></ref> The shrine's torii plaque now bears the inscriptions "Raiden Shrine (right)" and "Hikawa Shrine (left)," reflecting this historical merger.

In 1873, under the Modern Shrine Ranking System, Raiden Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社). In 1886, it absorbed the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|ja|氷川神社|12=draft}} previously managed by Sanmyōin as its bettō-ji.<ref name="jinja"></ref> The shrine's torii plaque now bears the inscriptions "Raiden Shrine (right)" and "Hikawa Shrine (left)," reflecting this historical merger.

6 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:雷電神社 (さいたま市) up to rev 94672273
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 雷電神社 |画像 = [[File:Raiden Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]][[今羽町]]154 |位置 = {{Coord|35|57|14.2|N|139|37|10.5|E|region:JP-11_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[雷神|大雷神]]、[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=雷電神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''雷電神社'''（らいでんじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ[[別当寺]]の「三明院」が[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]に創建されていることから、その頃に創建されたものと推測される。三明院は[[修験道]][[本山派]]の寺院であったが、[[明治]]初期の[[神仏分離]]により、[[廃寺]]に追い込まれた<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、324-325p</ref>。
[[1873年]]（明治6年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられ、[[1886年]]（明治19年）に三明院が別当寺を務めていた「氷川社」を合祀した<ref name="神社"></ref>。そのため、現在の当社の[[鳥居]]の[[扁額]]には「雷電神社（右）氷川神社（左）」と書かれている。
== 交通アクセス == *[[今羽駅]]より徒歩6分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年
{{Pref-stub|pref=埼玉県}} {{shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:らいてんしんしや}} [[Category:さいたま市の氷川神社]] [[Category:さいたま市北区の歴史]] [[Category:さいたま市北区の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (さいたま市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Raiden Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kita-ku, Saitama|qid=Q542542}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.953944444444446|N|139.61958333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q114641791]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine| image=Raiden Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Kita-ku, Saitama|Kita-ku, Saitama]], Saitama City, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines [[:en:Raijin|Raijin]] (Ōraijin) and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. However, its former {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, "Sanmyōin," was established during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], leading to speculation that the shrine was founded around the same time. In 1873, under the Modern Shrine Ranking System, Raiden Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社). In 1886, it absorbed the [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine]] previously managed by Sanmyōin as its bettō-ji.<ref name="jinja"></ref> The shrine's torii plaque now bears the inscriptions "Raiden Shrine (right)" and "Hikawa Shrine (left)," reflecting this historical merger.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (さいたま市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Raiden Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kita-ku, Saitama|WD=Q542542}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.953944444444446|N|139.61958333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q114641791]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine| image=Raiden Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Kita-ku, Saitama|Kita-ku, Saitama]], Saitama City, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines [[:en:Raijin|Raijin]] (Ōraijin) and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. However, its former {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, "Sanmyōin," was established during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], leading to speculation that the shrine was founded around the same time. Sanmyōin was a temple of the {{ill|Honzan-ha|ja|本山派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honzan-ha|1=Honzan-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520192}} sect of [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], but it was abolished during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]].<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998, pp. 324–325.</ref>
In 1873, under the Modern Shrine Ranking System, Raiden Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社). In 1886, it absorbed the [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine]] previously managed by Sanmyōin as its bettō-ji.<ref name="jinja"></ref> The shrine's torii plaque now bears the inscriptions "Raiden Shrine (right)" and "Hikawa Shrine (left)," reflecting this historical merger.
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot from [[:en:Komba Station|Kombu Station]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998.

Sanmyōin was a temple of the {{ill|Honzan-ha|ja|本山派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honzan-ha|1=Honzan-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520192}} sect of [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], but it was abolished during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]].<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998, pp. 324–325.</ref>
In 1873, under the Modern Shrine Ranking System, Raiden Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社). In 1886, it absorbed the [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine]] previously managed by Sanmyōin as its bettō-ji.<ref name="jinja"></ref> The shrine's torii plaque now bears the inscriptions "Raiden Shrine (right)" and "Hikawa Shrine (left)," reflecting this historical merger.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (さいたま市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (さいたま市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (さいたま市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Raiden Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kita-ku, Saitama|WD=Q542542}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.953944444444446|N|139.61958333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q114641791]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine| image=Raiden Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Kita-ku, Saitama|Kita-ku, Saitama]], Saitama City, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines [[:en:Raijin|Raijin]] (Ōraijin) and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. However, its former {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, "Sanmyōin," was established during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], leading to speculation that the shrine was founded around the same time. Sanmyōin was a temple of the {{ill|Honzan-ha|ja|本山派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honzan-ha|1=Honzan-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520192}} sect of [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]], but it was abolished during the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] reforms of the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]].<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998, pp. 324–325.</ref>
In 1873, under the Modern Shrine Ranking System, Raiden Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社). In 1886, it absorbed the [[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine]] previously managed by Sanmyōin as its bettō-ji.<ref name="jinja"></ref> The shrine's torii plaque now bears the inscriptions "Raiden Shrine (right)" and "Hikawa Shrine (left)," reflecting this historical merger.
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot from [[:en:Komba Station|Kombu Station]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (さいたま市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (さいたま市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (さいたま市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Raiden Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kita-ku, Saitama|WD=Q542542}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.953944444444446|N|139.61958333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q114641791]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine| image=Raiden Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kita-ku, Saitama|en|Kita-ku, Saitama|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, Saitama City, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Raijin|en|Raijin|WD=Q12808082|lt=Raijin}} (Ōraijin) and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. However, its former {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, "Sanmyōin," was established during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, leading to speculation that the shrine was founded around the same time. Sanmyōin was a temple of the {{ill|Honzan-ha|ja|本山派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honzan-ha|1=Honzan-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520192}} sect of {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō}}, but it was abolished during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} reforms of the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}.<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998, pp. 324–325.</ref>
In 1873, under the Modern Shrine Ranking System, Raiden Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社). In 1886, it absorbed the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|en|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|WD=Q703633|lt=Hikawa Shrine}} previously managed by Sanmyōin as its bettō-ji.<ref name="jinja"></ref> The shrine's torii plaque now bears the inscriptions "Raiden Shrine (right)" and "Hikawa Shrine (left)," reflecting this historical merger.
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot from {{ill|Komba Station|en|Komba Station|WD=Q11377372|lt=Kombu Station}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998.

In 1873, under the Modern Shrine Ranking System, Raiden Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社). In 1886, it absorbed the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|en|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|WD=Q703633|lt=Hikawa Shrine}} previously managed by Sanmyōin as its bettō-ji.<ref name="jinja"></ref> The shrine's torii plaque now bears the inscriptions "Raiden Shrine (right)" and "Hikawa Shrine (left)," reflecting this historical merger.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (さいたま市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (さいたま市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電神社 (さいたま市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Raiden Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kita-ku, Saitama|WD=Q542542|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.953944444444446|N|139.61958333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{infobox shinto shrine| image=Raiden Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG}} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社, Raiden Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kita-ku, Saitama|en|Kita-ku, Saitama|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita-ku, Saitama|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, Saitama City, Saitama Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Raijin|en|Raijin|WD=Q12808082|lt=Raijin|1=Raijin}} (Ōraijin) and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. However, its former {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, "Sanmyōin," was established during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, leading to speculation that the shrine was founded around the same time. Sanmyōin was a temple of the {{ill|Honzan-ha|ja|本山派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honzan-ha|1=Honzan-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520192}} sect of {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}}, but it was abolished during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} reforms of the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}.<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998, pp. 324–325.</ref>
In 1873, under the Modern Shrine Ranking System, Raiden Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社). In 1886, it absorbed the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|en|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|WD=Q703633|lt=Hikawa Shrine|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}} previously managed by Sanmyōin as its bettō-ji.<ref name="jinja"></ref> The shrine's torii plaque now bears the inscriptions "Raiden Shrine (right)" and "Hikawa Shrine (left)," reflecting this historical merger.
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot from {{ill|Komba Station|en|Komba Station|WD=Q11377372|lt=Kombu Station|1=Komba Station}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998.

It enshrines {{ill|Raijin|en|Raijin|WD=Q12808082|lt=Raijin|1=Raijin}} (Ōraijin) and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and is classified as a former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the Modern Shrine Ranking System.
== History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. However, its former {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, "Sanmyōin," was established during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, leading to speculation that the shrine was founded around the same time. Sanmyōin was a temple of the {{ill|Honzan-ha|ja|本山派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honzan-ha|1=Honzan-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520192}} sect of {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}}, but it was abolished during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} reforms of the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}.<ref name="jinja">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group, ''Saitama no Jinja: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama,'' Saitama Shrine Bureau, 1998, pp. 324–325.</ref>
In 1873, under the Modern Shrine Ranking System, Raiden Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社). In 1886, it absorbed the {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|en|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|WD=Q703633|lt=Hikawa Shrine|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}} previously managed by Sanmyōin as its bettō-ji.<ref name="jinja"></ref> The shrine's torii plaque now bears the inscriptions "Raiden Shrine (right)" and "Hikawa Shrine (left)," reflecting this historical merger.
== Access == * Approximately 6 minutes on foot from {{ill|Komba Station|en|Komba Station|WD=Q11377372|lt=Kombu Station|1=Komba Station}}.
